item.title: Oil Prices Don’t Reflect Scale of Supply Hit, Analysts Say
item.description: Oil prices don’t fully reflect the largest supply disruption ever, after the Iran war effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, some of the market’s top analysts said.

item.title: BOJ Flags Risk to Japan Bonds From Foreign Hedge Funds Unwinding
item.description: The Bank of Japan cautioned that a potential unwinding of positions by hedge funds globally may spread to Japan’s bond market.

item.title: Middle East Conflict Clouds Thai Banks Outlook as Profits Slip
item.description: Two major Thai banks struck a cautious tone on their 2026 outlook after reporting weak first-quarter net income, underscoring growing risks from Middle East conflict and a global energy shock.

item.title: Carlsberg Dumps Coca-Cola, Bets on Pepsi in Nordic Markets
item.description: Carlsberg A/S is preparing a major shift in its soft drinks strategy, replacing Coke with Pepsi across much of Northern Europe in a move set to reshape the region’s beverage market.

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook’s 15-Year Legacy by the Numbers
item.description: Fifteen years after succeeding Steve Jobs, Tim Cook is handing over the top leadership position at Apple Inc. to hardware specialist John Ternus. The outgoing chief executive officer, who’ll stay on as executive chairman, built up an unprecedented record of success over his tenure. Here are some of the numerical highlights.

item.title: Bawag Plans Dividend Cut, SRTs to Help Fund Permanent TSB Deal
item.description: Bawag Group AG will reduce investor payouts and rely on significant risk transfers to fund the €1.62 billion ($1.9 billion) acquisition of Ireland’s third-largest lender, Permanent TSB.

item.title: Swiss Watch Exports Dipped in March Amid Iran War and High Costs
item.description: Swiss watch exports slid in March as the industry faced disruptions in the Middle East and contended with high precious metal costs.

item.title: Swedish Supreme Court Clears Ex-Swedbank CEO in Dirty-Money Case
item.description: Swedbank AB’s former chief executive officer, Birgitte Bonnesen, was cleared of gross swindling by Sweden’s Supreme Court, overturning her previous prison sentence of 15 months in a case related to one of Europe’s largest money laundering scandals.

item.title: Swiss Real Wages Jump Most in Almost Two Decades
item.description: Swiss real wages grew 1.6% last year, the biggest increase since 2009, according to the national statistics office.

item.title: UN Agency Prepares Hormuz Evacuation Plan for Hundreds of Ships
item.description: The International Maritime Organization is working on an evacuation plan for hundreds of ships that have been stuck in the Persian Gulf since US and Israeli strikes on Iran began more than seven weeks ago, according to Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez said.

item.title: Rupee Slips as Traders Assess Impact of RBI’s FX Market Easing
item.description: The rupee edged lower as traders assessed a partial rollback of the central bank’s restrictions on currency market transactions, a move aimed at easing investor concerns about hedging.

item.title: Asia’s Largest Oil Buyers Are Running Low on Hormuz Alternatives
item.description: Asia’s largest oil buyers have been able to lean on workarounds to limit the impact of more than seven weeks of war in the Persian Gulf, shielding not only their own economies but those of neighbors competing for cargoes.

item.title: France Sees Up to €6 Billion Budget Impact From Iran War
item.description: The war in the Middle East will have an estimated impact on the French budget of as much as €6 billion ($7.1 billion), Finance Minister Roland Lescure said in an interview with RTL radio.

item.title: Mythos Access Must Be Granted on Level Playing Field, Nagel Says
item.description: Anthropic’s Mythos model should be shared with affected organizations to ensure a level playing field in assessing its uses and dangers, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Apple Bets New CEO John Ternus Will Bring Back Jobs-Era Decisiveness
item.description: When Apple Inc. announced Monday that longtime leader Tim Cook would be replaced by John Ternus, it published an image of the two executives walking side by side at the company’s campus in Cupertino, California.

item.title: China Bonds Set for Best Month Since October on Ample Cash
item.description: China’s benchmark bonds are heading for their best month since October, as abundant liquidity offsets concerns over upcoming debt supply.

item.title: Brevan Howard Plans Tokyo Office, to Hire Traders in Hub Boost
item.description: Brevan Howard Asset Management plans to open a Tokyo office this summer, joining the rush of global finance giants seeking to capitalize on trading opportunities in the Japanese market, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: AB Foods to Separate Its Budget Fashion Chain Primark
item.description: Associated British Foods Plc will separate budget apparel chain Primark from the rest of its business, in a breakup of one of the UK’s biggest conglomerates.

item.title: Poland Fines Luxury Car Trader for Illegal Exports to Russia
item.description: Poland has fined a local company 20 million zloty ($5.5 million) for intentionally violating European Union sanctions by selling luxury cars to Russia, the country’s tax authority said.

item.title: UK Job Cuts Suggest Companies Turning Cautious on Iran War Shock
item.description: UK businesses stepped up job cutting in March, a sign that the Iran war is causing fresh caution in the labor market after warnings Britain will be one of the hardest hit advanced economies from the energy shock.

item.title: Japanet Expands Its VC Fund After Bets on Anthropic, xAI Pay Off
item.description: Japanese home shopping company Japanet Holdings Co. is expanding its venture capital fund with San Jose-based Pegasus Tech Ventures Inc., following the success of early bets in SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic PBC and xAI.

item.title: India Bond Clearing House Said to Apply for European Recognition
item.description: India’s sovereign debt clearinghouse sent an application to European authorities to be recognized as a permitted trading counterparty, according to people familiar with the developments, potentially paving the way for lenders like Deutsche Bank AG and BNP Paribas SA to trade bonds more easily in the country.

item.title: Inox Clean Is Said to Revive Plan for Up to $1 Billion India IPO
item.description: Inox Clean Energy Ltd. has revived plans for an initial public offering that could raise as much as $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: FTSE 100 Poised to Rise as Iran Optimism Returns
RBI Flags Inflation Spillover Risks From Middle East Conflict
item.description: India’s central bank governor said the Middle East crisis could fuel persistent inflation if supply disruptions endure, given the country’s deep economic links to the region.

item.title: Data-Center Cooling Stocks Sink in China on Competition Concerns
item.description: Shares of Chinese liquid-cooling providers fell after a closely watched company reported earnings that missed expectations, sparking concerns over intensifying competition.

item.title: Indian Debt Funds Cut Hedges as Oil Risks Inflate Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Some Indian debt fund managers are cutting back on interest-rate hedges against their bond holdings, saying markets have already priced in an excessive rise in borrowing costs driven by a surge in oil prices.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Flag Risks for Kenya’s Shilling as War Rages On
item.description: Kenya’s shilling is among the most vulnerable currencies in Africa, according to strategists at major banks, who predict further weakness as elevated oil prices pile pressure on the economy.

item.title: Chinese Manufacturing Growth Overtaken by Finance Amid IPO Boom
item.description: Chinese manufacturing grew slower than finance for the first time in years, turbocharged by capital raised through share sales during a quarter that saw a surprise rebound in the economy.

item.title: Hong Kong Bourse Plans to Start Zero-Day Options in Early 2027
item.description: Hong Kong’s stock exchange is working on introducing options expiring within a day next year, joining a global trend toward shorter-dated bets that’s sent US derivatives volume ballooning.
